To do so, we rely on a combination of intellectual property registrations, assignment and nondisclosure agreements with team members and third parties, other contractual restrictions, and technological measures, among other safeguards.
  131 Table of Contents   For example, we hold several trademark registrations and pending trademark applications in the U.S.
and elsewhere, including material word marks and logos of AOL, Bending Spoons, Brightcove, Eventbrite, Evernote, Harvest, komoot, Remini, StreamYard, Vimeo, and WeTransfer.
We also have several issued patents and pending patent applications in the U.S.
and elsewhere.
We do not consider any individual patent or patent application to be material.
In addition, we maintain a portfolio of internet domain names that we use, or intend to use, in our business.
Real estate We are headquartered in Milan, Italy, where we occupy approximately 40,000 square feet of office space under a lease agreement that expires on November 30, 2030, and may be renewed for an additional six years.
On October 10, 2025, we entered into another lease agreement in Milan for approximately 100,000 square feet of office space, which expires on August 19, 2035, and may be renewed for an additional six years.
In London, U.K., we occupy approximately 39,000 square feet of office space under lease arrangements that expire in 2036.
We also lease or have service memberships for offices in Madrid, Spain, New York, U.S., and elsewhere.
We do not consider these lease or service membership agreements material.
We do not own any real estate.
We intend to expand our office footprint to support our growth, and believe that suitable additional or alternative space will be available on commercially reasonable terms as needed.
Regulation We are subject to the laws and regulations of various jurisdictions, including Italy, the E.U., the U.K., and the U.S., in areas such as privacy, data protection and security, intellectual property, artificial intelligence, consumer protection, advertising, product and content, competition, economic and trade sanctions, import and export controls, anti-bribery and anti-corruption, national security and foreign investment, sustainability and climate, employment and labor, and taxation.
The legal and regulatory frameworks applicable to our business differ across jurisdictions.
Some may be more restrictive than others or may impose overlapping or conflicting requirements.
Moreover, these laws and regulations may evolve, be interpreted, or be applied in ways that could adversely affect us.
Legal proceedings We are involved in, and may in the future be involved in, claims, legal proceedings, and regulatory investigations or inquiries arising in the ordinary course of business.
Although the outcome of any such matters cannot be predicted with certainty, we do not believe that the resolution of any currently pending matters, individually or in the aggregate, is likely to have a material adverse effect on our business.

  134 Table of Contents   Family relationships There are no family relationships among any of our executive officers or directors.
Composition of our board of directors The Italian Civil Code provides for three alternative corporate governance systems for a company's management and control, consisting of, respectively, (i) a board of directors and a board of statutory auditors, (ii) a management board and a supervisory board, or (iii) a board of directors and an audit committee ( comitato per il controllo sulla gestione ).
On April 23, 2026, in connection with the approval of our Bylaws and subject to and with effect from the effective date of this registration statement, we adopted the corporate governance system with a board of directors and an audit committee.
The board of directors will be elected by a shareholder meeting, and the audit committee will be, in turn, elected by the board of directors from among its members.
The board of directors that will take office subject to and with effect from the effective date of this registration statement will consist of nine members.
At least one third of the members of our board of directors, rounded up to the highest number, must meet the independence requirements set forth by our Bylaws and the Italian Civil Code.
Our board of directors has determined that Joshua Motta, Robert J.
Mylod, Jr., Donald D.
O'Neal, Steve Sinwell, and Paola Tagliavini do not have relationships that would interfere with the exercise of independent judgment in carrying out their responsibilities as directors and that each of these directors is an &#8220;independent&#8221; director as defined under Nasdaq rules, our Bylaws, and the Italian Civil Code.
Audit committee We expect that our board of directors that will take office subject to and with effect from the effective date of this registration statement will elect an audit committee.
The audit committee will oversee our accounting and financial reporting processes, the audits and integrity of our financial statements, adherence to sound management principles, the adequacy and effective functioning of our organizational, administrative, and accounting structure, compliance with applicable law and our Bylaws, and the qualifications and independence of our auditor.
The audit committee may perform additional activities upon the request of our board of directors.
Members of our audit committee must meet the independence requirements under Nasdaq independence rules, article 2396- septies of the Italian Civil Code, and the additional requirements of independence and financial expertise required by applicable law (including Rule 10A-3 of the Exchange Act).
Members of the executive committee (when present) and directors who hold delegated powers, special offices, or who perform management functions at Bending Spoons or at companies that control or are controlled by it, may not serve on the audit committee.
At least one member of the audit committee must be registered in the Italian register of auditors ( registro dei revisori legali ), and at least one member must qualify as an &#8220;audit committee financial expert&#8221; within the meaning of applicable SEC rules.
The audit committee will be governed by a charter that will be consistent and compliant with SEC and Nasdaq rules and applicable law.
The audit committee is expected to consist of Robert J.
Mylod, Jr., Donald D.
O'Neal, Steve Sinwell, and Paola Tagliavini.
Our board of directors has determined that each of Robert J.
Mylod, Jr., Donald D.
O'Neal, Steve Sinwell, and Paola Tagliavini satisfies the independence requirements under Nasdaq independence rules, article 2396- septies of the Italian Civil Code, and Rule 10A-3 under the Exchange Act.
Steve Sinwell is considered an &#8220;audit committee financial expert,&#8221; and Paola Tagliavini is registered in the Italian register of auditors ( registro dei revisori legali ).
Steve Sinwell will serve as chair of the audit committee.
Duties of directors Under Italian law, the board of directors is generally responsible for the management of the company.
The board may take all actions it deems useful and appropriate to achieve our corporate purpose, except for matters reserved for our shareholders under our Bylaws and applicable law.
Directors must generally act with care, in the company's interest, without pursuing personal interests, and on an informed basis.
  135 Table of Contents   The applicable standard of conduct is assessed on a case-by-case basis, taking into account, among other factors, the characteristics of the company, the specific tasks and responsibilities assigned to each director, and each director's experience and qualifications.
Directors are also subject to a number of statutory duties, including obligations relating to the maintenance of corporate books and records, the preparation and filing of annual financial statements, the convening of shareholder meetings when required, and the monitoring of the company's financial position.
To the extent permitted by our Bylaws and applicable law, the board of directors may delegate its authority and responsibility to one or more of its members or to an executive committee from among its members.
Election of directors Pursuant to our current bylaws, which will be amended and restated subject to and with effect from the effective date of this registration statement, all members of our board of directors are elected by the holders of our class A shares by a majority vote.
If, for two consecutive meetings, the members of our board of directors are not elected by the holders of our class A shares by a majority vote, they are instead elected at a shareholder meeting with the majorities required under applicable law.
The board of directors that will take office as of the effective date of this registration statement was elected in accordance with these provisions on May 28, 2026.
Any future election of a member of our board of directors will be governed by our Bylaws.
For additional information, see Description of share capital and bylaws .
Code of ethics We have adopted a code of ethics that addresses, among other things, conflicts of interest, compliance matters, and other company policies such as equal opportunity and non-discrimination standards.
Our code of ethics applies to all of our executive officers, directors, and other team members.
Upon our listing on Nasdaq, our code of ethics will be available on our website.
In addition, we intend to post on our website all disclosures that are required by applicable law or the Nasdaq rules concerning any amendments to, or waivers from, any provision of the code.
Foreign private issuer status As a foreign private issuer whose shares will be listed on Nasdaq, we will have the option to follow certain Italian corporate governance practices rather than certain Nasdaq requirements applicable to domestic issuers.
We intend to rely on this &#8220;foreign private issuer exemption&#8221; with respect to the following requirements: &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5605(b)(2), which requires that independent directors regularly meet in executive session, where only independent directors are present.
Our independent directors may choose to meet in executive session at their discretion.
&#8203; &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5605(d)(2), which requires that a listed company have a compensation committee composed of entirely independent directors.
Under Italian law, we are not required to establish internal board committees apart from the audit committee (comitato per il controllo sulla gestione) .
&#8203; &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5605(e), which requires that director nominees must either be selected, or recommended for the board's selection, either by independent directors constituting a majority of the board's independent directors in a vote in which only independent directors participate, or a nomination committee comprised solely of independent directors.
Under Italian law, we are not required to establish internal board committees apart from the audit committee (comitato per il controllo sulla gestione) .
&#8203; &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5620(c) regarding quorum requirements applicable to meetings of shareholders.
Such quorum requirements are not required under Italian law.
The quorum requirements as set forth in our Bylaws and Italian law apply.
&#8203; &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5635(a) regarding shareholder approval requirements in connection with an acquisition of shares or assets of another company where any director, officer, or substantial shareholder has a 5% or greater interest in the target company or the consideration to be received, or that involves the issuance of 20% or more of the acquirer's shares or voting rights.
&#8203;   136 Table of Contents   &#8226; Nasdaq Rule 5635(c) regarding shareholder approval requirements for the issuance of securities in connection with a stock option or purchase plan that is established or materially amended, or other equity compensation arrangement that is established or materially amended.
&#8203; Although we may rely on certain home country corporate governance practices, we will be required to comply with Nasdaq's Notification of Noncompliance requirement (Nasdaq Rule 5625) and the Voting Rights requirement (Nasdaq Rule 5640).
Further, we must have an audit committee that satisfies Nasdaq Rule 5605(c)(3), which addresses audit committee responsibilities and authority and requires that the audit committee consist of members who meet the independence requirements of Nasdaq Rule 5605(c)(2)(A)(ii).
Except as stated above, we intend to comply with the rules generally applicable to U.S.
domestic issuers listed on Nasdaq.
We may in the future decide to use other foreign private issuer exemptions with respect to some or all of the other Nasdaq listing requirements.
Upon the effectiveness of this registration statement, we will be subject to both the provisions of the Italian Civil Code that apply to joint stock companies and to companies listed on a regulated market (societ&#224; che fanno ricorso al mercato del capitale di rischio) .
As described in more detail in Description of share capital and bylaws , these provisions differ in a number of ways from those applicable to U.S.
domestic issuers under the rules of Nasdaq.
Following our home country governance practices, as opposed to the requirements that would otherwise apply to a company listed on Nasdaq, may provide less protection than is accorded to investors under Nasdaq listing requirements applicable to domestic issuers.
We intend to take all actions necessary for us to maintain compliance as a foreign private issuer under the applicable corporate governance requirements of SOX, the rules adopted by the SEC, and Nasdaq listing standards.
As a foreign private issuer, our officers and directors will be subject to Section 16(a) reporting requirements on Forms 3, 4, and 5 with regard to the disclosure of their beneficial ownership and changes in ownership of our securities.
However, these officers and directors will not be subject to short-swing profit and short sale reporting obligations under Sections 16(b) and (c) of the Exchange Act.
Executive officer and director compensation For 2025, the total compensation for our executive officers and directors was $10.8 million, of which $2.2 million was paid in cash, and $8.6 million represents the grant date fair value of equity compensation earned during 2025 as calculated in accordance with Accounting Standards Codification (&#8220;ASC&#8221;) Topic 718.
See Equity compensation expense in Note 11 to our audited consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this prospectus for the assumptions used in calculating this amount.
The total amount set aside or accrued by us to provide pension, retirement, or similar benefits (trattamento di fine rapporto) to our executive officers and directors with respect to 2025 was $0.2 million.
Executive officer agreements We have entered into employment agreements with our executive officers other than Luca Ferrari, our chief executive officer, and Francesco Patarnello, our head of business acquisitions.
These agreements follow the applicable Italian industry-wide collective bargaining agreements for matters not specifically covered, such as notice periods and holiday entitlement.
Our agreements with such executive officers also contain customary provisions regarding confidentiality of information and assignment of intellectual property rights.
However, the enforceability of such provisions may be limited under applicable law.
Equity compensation Stock option plans We have granted stock options to purchase class X-1 and class X-2 shares to eligible team members under our stock option plans (as amended from time to time, the &#8220;Stock Option Plans&#8221;).
Upon the effectiveness of our Bylaws and the completion of this offering, all outstanding stock options granted under the Stock Option Plans will convert into stock options to purchase our ordinary shares.
The material terms of our Stock Option Plans are summarized below.
  137 Table of Contents   Eligibility and administration Our employees, consultants, and directors are eligible to receive stock option grants under the Stock Option Plans.
Participants may elect to receive a portion of their cash compensation in the form of stock options for a specified period.
The Stock Option Plans are administered by our board of directors.
Grants and vesting Stock options vest over time in accordance with their terms and may be exercised during designated exercise windows.
The Stock Option Plans provide for an exercise window in connection with a listing event and, before a listing event, an annual exercise window and an exercise window in connection with a change of control (each as defined in the Stock Option Plans).
After a listing event, there will be an exercise window in connection with a change of control and other exercise windows at such times as our board of directors determines in a manner consistent with the insider trading policy and the procedures that will be adopted in connection with the listing.
Exercise is subject to payment of the applicable exercise price and required tax withholding.
In the case of a change of control or listing event, exercise is also conditioned upon the completion of the transaction.
Under the Stock Option Plans, following the expiration of an exercise window in connection with a change of control or listing event, we may elect to redeem vested stock options and eligible unvested options that remain unexercised at a price equal to the per-share value determined in the applicable transaction, less the exercise price.
For stock options granted instead of cash compensation, the redemption price may not be less than the amount of compensation waived in connection with the grant.
This offering will constitute a listing event under the Stock Option Plans.
Where a participant is a service provider of one of our subsidiaries, we will (i) transfer to the subsidiary the shares necessary for the subsidiary to fulfill its obligations under any stock option grant to which the subsidiary is a party, and (ii) deliver such shares to the participant on behalf of the subsidiary.
No additional stock options will be granted under the Stock Option Plans after June 30, 2026, and any stock options outstanding under the Stock Option Plans will expire on June 30, 2036.
As of June 15, 2026, stock options to purchase an aggregate of 36,333,777 ordinary shares were outstanding under the Stock Option Plans at a weighted average exercise price of $0.281 per share (based on an exchange rate of $1.1607 per &euro;1.00, as of June 15, 2026, the euro-denominated weighted average exercise price is &euro;0.24 per share).
The equity interests held by executive officers and directors pursuant to the arrangements described in this section are included in Principal and selling shareholders below.
Warrants We have granted warrants to members of our and our subsidiaries' boards of directors.
Similar to our Stock Option Plans, eligible directors may elect to receive all or a portion of their cash compensation in the form of warrants for a specified period.
The warrants generally vest on a monthly basis and may be exercised in connection with a change of control or listing event.
Upon exercise, each warrant entitles the holder to purchase five shares.
As of June 15, 2026, warrants to purchase an aggregate of 91,730 ordinary shares with an exercise price of $1.240 per share (based on an exchange rate of $1.1607 per &euro;1.00, as of June 15, 2026, the euro-denominated weighted average exercise price is &euro;1.07 per share) were outstanding.
2026 equity compensation plan The shareholder meeting held on June 6, 2026, approved our 2026 equity compensation plan (the &#8220;2026 Equity Compensation Plan&#8221;) which became effective as of that date.
Under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan, we may make cash and equity-based grants to team members who are eligible service providers.
As of June 15, 2026, no grants have been made under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan.
The material terms of the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan are summarized below.
Eligibility and administration Our employees, consultants, and directors are eligible to receive cash and equity-based grants under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan.
Participants may elect to receive all or part of their cash compensation in the form of grants under the   138 Table of Contents   2026 Equity Compensation Plan for a specified period, if permitted and in accordance with the terms determined by the Administrator (as defined below).
The 2026 Equity Compensation Plan is expected to be administered by our board of directors, subject to authorized delegations of authority to a committee of our board of directors or to one or more team members who are eligible service providers in accordance with the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan and applicable law (collectively, the &#8220;Administrator&#8221;).
The Administrator has the authority to make all determinations and interpretations under, and adopt rules for the administration of, the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan, subject to its terms and conditions, including setting the terms and conditions of all grants, designating participants, and establishing any vesting, performance criteria, or other restrictions applicable to grants.
Limitation on grants and shares available The maximum number of shares available for grant under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan is 51,000,000 shares which will consist of ordinary shares upon the effectiveness of our Bylaws and the completion of this offering.
The shares covered by grants that expire, lapse, are forfeited, or are canceled or terminated without being exercised or settled will again be available for grant under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan.
Grants assigned under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan in assumption of, or in substitution for, outstanding equity grants previously assigned by an entity in connection with a corporate transaction will not reduce the shares authorized for grant under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan.
Grants and vesting The 2026 Equity Compensation Plan provides for the grant of stock options, restricted stock, restricted stock units, stock appreciation rights, and other grants, including cash grants and other grants denominated in, linked to, or derived from our shares.
Grants will be set forth in grant agreements, which will specify the terms and conditions of the grants, including any applicable vesting and payment terms.
A brief description of each grant type follows: &#8226; Stock options entitle their holder to purchase our shares in the future at an exercise price set by the Administrator.
The term of a stock option may not be longer than ten years from the grant date.
&#8203; &#8226; Stock appreciation rights entitle their holder, upon exercise, to receive from us an amount equal to the appreciation of the shares subject to the grant between the grant date and the exercise date.
The term of a stock appreciation right may not be longer than ten years from the grant date.
&#8203; &#8226; Restricted stock units are contractual promises to deliver our shares in the future, which may also remain forfeitable unless specified conditions are met.
&#8203; &#8226; Restricted stock is a grant of nontransferable shares that remain forfeitable unless specified conditions are met.
&#8203; &#8226; Other grants include grants of cash or shares.
Other grants can be denominated in, linked to, or derived from our shares, and may remain forfeitable unless specified conditions are met.
Grants of cash are cash bonuses subject to performance goals or other terms and conditions.
&#8203; Vesting conditions determined by the Administrator may apply to each grant and may include continued service, performance, and other conditions.
Certain transactions The Administrator has broad discretion to take action under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan, and to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of existing and future grants, to prevent the dilution or enlargement of intended benefits and to facilitate corporate transactions affecting our shares.
This includes canceling grants for cash or property, accelerating vesting, providing for the assumption or substitution of grants by a successor entity, adjusting the number and type of shares available, and replacing or terminating grants under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan for cash or other consideration (or without payment if their value is zero or less).
In the event of certain non-reciprocal transactions with our shareholders known as equity restructurings, the Administrator will make equitable adjustments to the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan and outstanding grants as it deems appropriate.
  139 Table of Contents   Amendment of grants, claw-back provisions, transferability, and participant payments The Administrator may amend, modify, or terminate the terms of the grants, establish sub-plans, and adjust other terms and conditions of grants to assign grants, subject to applicable law and stock exchange rules.
Where a participant is a service provider of one of our subsidiaries, we will (i) transfer to the subsidiary the shares necessary for the subsidiary to fulfill its obligations under any grant agreement to which the subsidiary is a party, and (ii) deliver such shares to the participant on behalf of the subsidiary.
The Administrator may not, without the approval of our shareholders, reduce the exercise price of any stock option, stock appreciation rights, or other grants, or cancel any stock option or stock appreciation right in exchange for cash, other grants, or stock options or stock appreciation rights with an exercise price per share that is less than the exercise price per share of the original stock options or stock appreciation rights.
All grants will be subject to any claw-back or recoupment policy we may adopt.
Grants under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan are generally non-transferable and exercisable only by the participant, with limited exceptions for the laws of descent and distribution.
Plan amendment and termination Our board of directors may amend, suspend, or terminate the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan at any time.
However, no amendment may materially and adversely affect any outstanding grant without the affected participant's consent except when made in connection with a corporate transaction, and shareholder approval will be required for any amendment that increases the number of shares available under the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan or as otherwise required by applicable law.
No grant may be assigned pursuant to the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan after the tenth anniversary of the effective date of the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan.
Insurance and indemnification On or before the time of effectiveness of this registration statement, we will enter into indemnification agreements with our executive officers and directors.
These indemnification agreements will require us to indemnify our executive officers and directors to the fullest extent permitted by law.
Under Italian law, indemnification is not permitted, among other cases, for acts or omissions involving willful misconduct or gross negligence, or for liability of an executive officer or director toward the company.
Insofar as indemnification of liabilities arising under the Securities Act may be permitted to executive officers and directors or persons controlling us pursuant to the foregoing provisions, we have been informed that, in the opinion of the SEC, such indemnification is against public policy as expressed in the Securities Act and is therefore unenforceable.
We maintain insurance policies relating to certain liabilities that our executive officers and directors may incur in such capacity.
In any underwriting agreement we enter into in connection with the sale of the ordinary shares being registered hereby, the underwriters will agree to indemnify, under certain conditions, us, our executive officers, directors, and persons who control us within the meaning of the Securities Act against certain liabilities.

Description of the transactions On September 10, 2025, we entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Vimeo, Inc.
and the acquisition was completed on November 24, 2025.
On October 29, 2025, we entered into a definitive agreement to acquire AOL Holdco I LLC, and the acquisition was completed on January 2, 2026.
On December 1, 2025, we entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Eventbrite, Inc.
and the acquisition was completed on March 10, 2026.
Each of the acquisitions of AOL Holdco I LLC and Vimeo, Inc.
is considered a significant acquisition pursuant to Rule 3-05 of Regulation S-X.
See Note 5 to our audited consolidated financial statements as of and for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2024, and 2025, and Note 5 to our unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements as of March 31, 2026 and for the three months ended March 31, 2025 and 2026 for a description of our acquisitions.
As of the date of this prospectus, AOL Holdco I LLC, Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc.
are wholly owned indirect subsidiaries of Bending Spoons S.p.A.

&#8203; &#8203; $ 0.04 &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; &#8203; $ (0.02) &#8203; &#8203; For further information, please see the accompanying notes to the unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information   144 Table of Contents   Notes to unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information 1.
  Basis of presentation The unaudited pro forma condensed combined income statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 and for three months ended March 31, 2026 combine the historical consolidated income statement of AOL Holdco II LLC, Bending Spoons S.p.A., Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc.
and has been prepared as if the acquisitions and the related financing described under Unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information&#8201;-&#8201;Description of the transactions had occurred on January 1, 2025, the beginning of the periods presented.
This unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information is being provided for compliance purposes only and does not represent our actual financial position or results of operations had the acquisitions and the related financing described under Unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information&#8201;-&#8201;Description of the transactions occurred on the date specified, nor does it project results of operations or financial position for any future period or date.
The acquisition method of accounting for business combinations was used in accordance with ASC 805, Business Combinations .
Based on the acquisition method of accounting, the consideration paid for each acquisition is allocated to its assets and liabilities based on their fair value as of the date of the completion of the acquisition.
Under ASC 805, acquisition-related transaction costs and acquisition-related restructuring charges are not included as components of consideration transferred but are accounted for as expenses in the period in which the costs are incurred.
The process for estimating the fair values of identifiable intangible assets requires the use of significant estimates and assumptions, including estimating future cash flows and developing appropriate discount rates.
The excess purchase price over the estimated amounts of identifiable assets of the subsidiaries acquired as of the effective date of the acquisitions were allocated to goodwill in accordance with the accounting guidance.
The purchase price allocation and valuation of each of the acquisitions of AOL Holdco I LLC, Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc.
are based on preliminary estimates, subject to final adjustments.
Accordingly, the purchase accounting in the unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information is preliminary and will be adjusted upon completion of the final valuation.
Such adjustments could be material.
For purposes of measuring the estimated fair value of the assets acquired as reflected in the unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information, in accordance with the applicable accounting guidance, we established a framework for measuring fair values.
The applicable accounting guidance defines fair value as the price that would be received to sell an asset in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date.
Market participants are assumed to be buyers and sellers in the principal or most advantageous market for the asset or liability.
Additionally, under the applicable accounting guidance, fair value measurements for an asset assume the highest and best use of that asset by market participants.
As a result, we may be required to value assets of the acquired subsidiaries at fair value measures that do not reflect our intended use of those assets.
Use of different estimates and judgments could yield different results.
The purchase price allocations for the acquisitions of AOL Holdco I LLC, Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc.
are preliminary and have been prepared based on management's initial estimates and available information as of the date of this prospectus.
For each of AOL Holdco I LLC, Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc., the following items remain subject to finalization: (i) the identification and valuation of acquired intangible assets, including tradenames, developed technology, and customer relationships; and (ii) the resulting allocation to goodwill and the recognition of any associated deferred tax liabilities.
The final fair values assigned to these assets and liabilities may differ materially from the preliminary amounts reflected in the unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information.
In order to finalize the purchase price allocations, we need to complete detailed valuation analyses for the identified intangible assets of each acquired entity, including the receipt of final third-party valuation reports.
In accordance with ASC 805-10-25-15, we will finalize the purchase price allocation for each acquisition within twelve months from the respective acquisition date.
Any measurement period adjustments identified during this period will be recognized in the reporting period in which they are determined.
  145 Table of Contents   The final allocations could differ materially from the preliminary amounts currently used in the pro forma adjustments, primarily as a result of changes in the fair values assigned to acquired intangible assets, revisions to goodwill, and adjustments to deferred tax positions.
Accordingly, the actual amortization charges and other adjustments reflected in pro forma following finalization may differ from those presented herein.
The unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information does not account for the cost of any restructuring activities or impact of any synergies resulting from the acquisitions of AOL Holdco I LLC, Eventbrite, Inc., and Vimeo, Inc., other costs relating to the integration of the acquired companies, or other prior acquisitions by Bending Spoons.
We and each acquired company have not had any historical relationship prior to the respective acquisition date.
Accordingly, no pro forma adjustments were required to eliminate activities between Bending Spoons and the acquired companies.

  Financing transaction-related adjustments Financing adjustments to the income statement Pro forma adjustment to interest expense, net reflects the estimated impact of the financing assumed to fund the acquisitions, as if such financing had been incurred on January 1, 2025.
For purposes of the pro forma financial information, the acquisitions are assumed to have been financed using certain debt financings raised from November 2025 onward, with such financings treated as if they had been incurred on January 1, 2025.
The related pro forma interest adjustment reflects the application of the relevant contractual interest rates, including the effect of associated hedging arrangements.
The financing arrangements considered in the financing transaction-related adjustments are summarized below.

historical consolidated financial statements as of and for 2025.
Accordingly, the related hedging effects have been reflected in the calculation of the pro forma interest expense adjustment with a fixed interest rate.
The three term loans entered into during 2026 (USD TLA, USD TLB, and EUR TLB) were not subject to completed hedge accounting as of the relevant reference date.
Accordingly, the pro forma interest expense adjustment related to such term loans has been calculated on an unhedged basis for the entire period presented.
A sensitivity analysis on interest expense has been performed to assess the effect of an eighth of a percent change of hypothetical interest on debt financing.
This change would cause a corresponding increase in the interest expense of approximately $10 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, and of approximately $0.1 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.

&#8203;   153 Table of Contents   10.
  Pro forma earnings per share The following table presents historical and pro forma basic and diluted earnings (loss) per share from continuing operations attributable to the controlling interest for the year ended December 31, 2025, and for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
The per-share figures reflect the stock split and the Share Conversion that was approved on April 23, 2026, and the reverse stock split that was approved on May 28, 2026.
For additional information on the stock split, the reverse stock split, and the Share Conversion, see Description of share capital and bylaws.
Pro forma earnings per share have been calculated by dividing pro forma net income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to the controlling interest by weighted-average shares outstanding during the period.
The pro forma net income (loss) reflects the adjustments described in the accompanying notes, including acquisition transaction-related adjustments and financing transaction-related adjustments, as if the acquisitions and the related financing had occurred on January 1, 2025.
The weighted-average shares outstanding used in the computation of pro forma basic and diluted earnings (loss) per share is based on the historical number of shares outstanding of the registrant for the relevant period, as no shares were issued or are assumed to be issued in connection with the acquisitions.
Accordingly, no adjustments have been made to the historical weighted average number of shares outstanding in calculating pro forma earnings per share.

The Share Conversion does not affect the computation of pro forma basic or diluted earnings per share because (i) all our classes of shares have the same participating rights in earnings, dividends, and distributions, such that basic and diluted earnings per share are identical across all classes, and (ii) the total number of shares outstanding does not change, as the Share Conversion is effected on a 1-for-1 basis with no shares issued or cancelled.
The Bylaws do not alter economic participation rights or the share count.
This pro forma earnings per share information does not give effect to this offering or the issuance of any shares in connection with it.
  154 Table of Contents &#8203;   Principal and selling shareholders The following table sets forth information relating to the beneficial ownership of our ordinary shares and class A shares as of May 29, 2026 (i) prior to the completion of this offering and (ii) as adjusted to reflect the sale of our ordinary shares in this offering by: &#8226; Each person, or group of affiliated persons, known by us to beneficially own 5% or more of our outstanding ordinary shares &#8203; &#8226; Each Selling Shareholder &#8203; &#8226; Each of our executive officers and our directors &#8203; &#8226; Our executive officers and our directors as a group &#8203; For further information regarding material transactions between us and principal shareholders, see Certain relationships and related party transactions.
The number of ordinary shares and class A shares beneficially owned by each entity, person, executive officer, or director is determined in accordance with the rules of the SEC, and the information is not necessarily indicative of beneficial ownership for any other purpose.
Under such rules, beneficial ownership includes any shares over which the individual has sole or shared voting power or investment power as well as any shares that the individual has the right to acquire within 60 days of May 29, 2026, through the exercise of any option, warrant, or other right.
Except as otherwise indicated, and subject to applicable community property laws, each person named in the table has sole voting and investment power with respect to all ordinary shares or class A shares held by that person.
In the table below, the percentage total voting power represents voting power with respect to all ordinary shares and class A shares, as a single class.
Each ordinary share will be entitled to one vote per share, and each class A share will be entitled to five votes per share.
Holders of our ordinary shares and class A shares generally vote together as a single class, unless otherwise required by our Bylaws or applicable law.
For additional information, see Description of share capital and bylaws .
The percentage of shares beneficially owned before the offering is computed on the basis of 290,579,985 of our outstanding ordinary shares and 310,240,685 of our outstanding class A shares, each as of May 29, 2026, after giving effect to the Share Conversion.
The percentage of shares beneficially owned after the offering is based on the number of our ordinary shares to be outstanding after this offering, including the ordinary shares that the Selling Shareholders are selling in this offering, and the number of our class A shares to be outstanding after this offering.
Ordinary shares or class A shares that a person has the right to acquire within 60 days of May 29, 2026, are deemed outstanding for purposes of computing the percentage ownership of the person holding such rights but are not deemed outstanding for purposes of computing the percentage ownership of any other person, except with respect to the percentage ownership of all executive officers and directors as a group.
As of May 29, 2026, after giving effect to the Share Conversion, we had 108 holders of record of our ordinary shares in the U.S., holding in the aggregate 104,887,845, or 36.10%, of our outstanding ordinary shares.
Unless otherwise indicated below, the address for each beneficial owner listed is Via Nino Bonnet 10, 20154 Milan, Italy.

&#8203;   157 Table of Contents &#8203;   Certain relationships and related party transactions This section contains summaries of certain transactions and relationships with our directors, executive officers, and certain of our shareholders since January 1, 2023.
Because these descriptions are only summaries of the applicable agreements, they do not necessarily contain all of the information that investors may find useful.
For a complete description of the relevant compensation arrangements, see &#8220;Management.&#8221; Note regarding stock splits and reverse stock splits Unless otherwise indicated, share and stock option amounts in this section are presented on the basis in effect at the time of the relevant transaction and have not been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 20-for-1 stock split that was approved on March 13, 2024, the 10-for-1 stock split that was approved on April 23, 2026, or the 1-for-2 reverse stock split that was approved on May 28, 2026.
Transactions with executive officers and directors prior to this offering Luca Ferrari Luca Ferrari serves as our chief executive officer and chair of our board of directors.
In 2022, Mr.
Ferrari received a gross bonus of &euro;824,520 from us, which he used to purchase 8,500 class E shares, later converted into class X-1 shares, through a capital increase on December 19, 2022.
In connection with this purchase, Mr.
Ferrari entered into a shareholder agreement with the then-existing holders of class B and class A shares pursuant to which he committed to transfer such shares to us, individuals identified by us, or the holders of class A shares by December 31, 2027, and transfer to us, as a non-refundable capital contribution, the proceeds from the sale of such shares.
This shareholder agreement was terminated in April 2026.
Mr.
Ferrari retained ownership of 644,175 class X-1 shares.
In 2024, Mr.
Ferrari received a two-day short-term loan of &euro;1,119,999.68 from us, representing an advance of the tax due on the sale of some of his shares.
This loan was repaid in full and is no longer outstanding.
Francesco Patarnello Francesco Patarnello serves as our head of business acquisitions and vice chair of our board of directors.
In 2024, Mr.
Patarnello received a two-day short-term loan of &euro;1,119,999.68 from us, representing an advance of the tax due on the sale of some of his shares.
This loan was repaid in full and is no longer outstanding.
Matteo Danieli Matteo Danieli serves as a member of our board of directors.
In 2024, Mr.
Danieli received a two-day short-term loan of &euro;1,087,994 from us, representing an advance of the tax due on the sale of some of his shares.
This loan was repaid in full and is no longer outstanding.
Leah Schwartz Leah Schwartz serves as a member of our board of directors.
Pursuant to the arrangements described in Management - Warrants , Ms.
Schwartz was granted 5,735 warrants in December 2024 at a discount, which was applied equally to the grants to all similarly situated team members.
These warrants were exercised in December 2025 and we paid Ms.
Schwartz $257,526.53 in cash in December 2025 to cover certain costs incurred in connection with the exercise of such warrants.
As compensation for her services as a director in 2025, we granted Ms.
Schwartz 8,014 class X-1 treasury shares, which were automatically converted into class X-2 shares upon the transfer of the shares, free of charge in December 2025.
Pursuant to the arrangements described in Management - Warrants , Ms.
Schwartz was granted 3,549 warrants in January 2026 at a discount, which was applied equally to the grants to all similarly situated team members.
These warrants currently remain unexercised.
  158 Table of Contents   Ms.
Schwartz also serves as a managing director of Allen & Company LLC, a financial advisor and shareholder of ours, and she is a member of Allen Operations, LLC (Allen & Company LLC's parent entity), which is also one of our shareholders.
Allen & Company LLC holds 2,895,200 class X-2 shares which represent 0.48% of our outstanding shares, and Allen Operations, LLC holds 2,800,000 class X-1 shares which represent 0.47% of our outstanding shares.
In February 2023, Ms.
Schwartz purchased 1,200 class E shares, later converted into class X-1 shares, through a capital increase for &euro;90,108 in an arm's length transaction alongside Allen Operations, LLC and other Allen & Company LLC team members.
In April 2023, Ms.
Schwartz, in her capacity as managing director of Allen & Company LLC, signed a financial advisory engagement letter with us on behalf of Allen & Company LLC (the &#8220;Engagement Letter&#8221;), pursuant to which Allen & Company LLC provides strategic and financial advisory services to us.
In connection with an equity financing completed in the first quarter of 2024, Allen & Company LLC received an advisory fee of $874,227 in cash and 27,200 class X-3 treasury shares, which were automatically converted into class X-2 shares upon the transfer of the shares, of which 4,080 class X-2 shares were attributed to Ms.
Schwartz.
Effective July 23, 2025, Allen & Company LLC temporarily suspended the services provided under the Engagement Letter in connection with the acquisitions of Vimeo, Inc.
and Eventbrite, Inc.
Such services have been resumed following the completion of the acquisition of Eventbrite, Inc.

  160 Table of Contents &#8203;   Description of share capital and bylaws The following is a summary of certain information about our shares, our Bylaws, and applicable Italian law.
This summary is not intended to be complete and may not contain all the information that may be relevant to a decision to purchase our ordinary shares.
For further information, investors should refer to our Bylaws and applicable Italian law.
We are a joint stock company ( societ&#224; per azioni ) incorporated in Italy.
Our corporate affairs are governed by our Bylaws, the Italian Civil Code, and other applicable laws.
During the past three years, our share capital and bylaws have been amended as follows.
Where amounts are denominated in euros, they have been converted into U.S.
dollars at the exchange rate published by the European Central Bank as of the date of the shareholder meeting approving the relevant share capital increase.
On February 25, 2022, our shareholders approved a share capital increase for no consideration with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;1,697.76 ($1,904.21).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 84,888 class E shares (later converted into class X-1 shares) that were reserved for the implementation of certain equity compensation plans.
Subscriptions were to be completed by December 31, 2026.
The share capital increase was amended on March 13, 2024, in the context of the March 2024 stock split described below.
The maximum aggregate nominal amount of, and the maximum number of shares authorized by, the unused portion of the share capital increase were amended to &euro;12,316 ($13,813.63) and 1,231,600, respectively.
On April 23, 2026, our shareholders canceled the unused portion as of that date.
On November 24, 2022, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;1,080 ($1,124.60) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;4,053,780 ($4,221,201.11).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 54,000 class E shares.
Subscriptions were to be completed by March 31, 2023.
The increase was partially executed and 41,104 class E shares (later converted into class X-1 shares) were issued at a subscription price of &euro;75.09 ($78.19) per share.
On April 6, 2023, our shareholders approved a share capital increase for no consideration with an aggregate nominal amount of &euro;15,000 ($16,372.50).
The increase authorized the issuance of 750,000 class G treasury shares (later converted into class X-3 shares) that were reserved for the implementation of certain equity compensation plans.
On May 10, 2023, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights, which was amended on March 13, 2024, in the context of the March 2024 stock split described below.
The share capital increase was issued (adjusted to reflect the March 2024 stock split) with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;8,000 ($8,760.00) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;5,999,200 ($6,569,124.00).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 800,000 class X-1 shares (adjusted to reflect the March 2024 stock split).
Subscriptions were to be completed by April 20, 2028.
This share capital increase was reserved for certain warrants granted to a strategic advisor under the warrant terms approved by our board of directors on April 20, 2023 (and amended on July 24, 2023, and December 18, 2025).
The increase was fully executed on March 12, 2026, by issuing all of the authorized 800,000 class X-1 shares (later converted into class X-2 shares) to the strategic advisor at a subscription price of &euro;7.509 ($8.22) per share.
On July 27, 2023, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights, with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;7,561.02 ($8,411.63) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;61,497,556.17 ($68,416,031.24).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 378,051 class C shares, to be issued in two tranches, the subscription of which was to be completed by August 31, 2023, and October 31, 2023, respectively.
The increase was partially executed, and 351,997 class C shares were issued at a subscription price of &euro;162.69 ($180.99) per share.
On September 29, 2023, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;1,400 ($1,483.16) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;11,386,900 ($12,063,281.86).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 70,000 class X-2 shares.
Subscriptions were to be completed by November 17, 2023.
The increase was partially executed, and 56,360 class X-2 shares were issued at a subscription price of &euro;162.69 ($172.35) per share.
On January 9, 2024, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;7,653.06 ($8,372.45) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;149,992,322.94 ($164,091,601.30).
  161 Table of Contents   The increase authorized the issuance of up to 382,653 class B shares, class C shares, class X-2 shares, or a combination of the foregoing, subject to the classes of shares held by the subscribing shareholders.
Subscriptions were to be completed by February 29, 2024.
This share capital increase was partially executed, and 23,886 class B shares, 217,971 class C shares, and 120,704 class X-2 shares (117,188 of which were subsequently converted into class C shares) were issued at a subscription price of &euro;392 ($428.85) per share.
On March 13, 2024, our shareholders approved a 20-for-1 stock split through a share capital increase for no consideration with an aggregate nominal amount of &euro;1,159,347.06 ($1,268,209.75).
The increase authorized the issuance of 61,771,717 class A shares, 7,494,664 class B shares, 18,258,031 class C shares, 19,348,707 class X-1 shares, 1,709,259 class X-2 shares, and 13,793,145 class X-3 shares with no par value.
The share capital increase was executed, and a total of 122,375,523 shares were issued to existing shareholders for no consideration and in proportion to their holdings as of the issuance date, with a corresponding reduction in the implicit par value per share from &euro;0.02 to &euro;0.01.
On October 30, 2024, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;18,800 ($20,332.20) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;42,036,800 ($45,462,799.20).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 1,880,000 class X-2 shares.
Subscriptions were to be completed by December 31, 2024.
This share capital increase was partially executed, and 1,788,109 class X-2 shares (which were converted into class C shares upon the resolution of a shareholder meeting) were issued at a subscription price of &euro;22.37 ($24.19) per share (&euro;447.40 or $483.86 without giving effect to the March 2024 stock split).
On October 30, 2024, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights, with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;852 ($921.44), plus a share premium.
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 85,200 class X-1 shares, class X-2 shares, or a combination of the two.
Subscriptions are to be completed by December 31, 2034.
The share capital increase is reserved for certain warrants granted to some of our directors under the warrant terms approved by our board of directors on November 21, 2024 (and amended on December 18, 2025).
On December 23, 2025, the share capital increase was partially executed, and a total of 11,470 class X-2 shares were issued to some of our directors at a subscription price of &euro;1.57 ($1.70) per share (&euro;31.40 or $33.96 without giving effect to the March 2024 stock split).
The share capital increase was amended on April 23, 2026, and on May 28, 2026, in the context of the April 2026 stock split and the May 2026 reverse stock split described below.
The maximum aggregate nominal amount of, and the maximum number of shares authorized by, the unused portion of the share capital increase were amended to &euro;7,373 ($7,973.90) and 368,650, respectively.
On September 17, 2025, our shareholders approved an amendment to our bylaws to, among other things, change the number of votes granted by each class A share.
Under our current bylaws, each class A share grants either one or five votes at our ordinary and extraordinary shareholder meetings, depending on certain conditions as specified in our current bylaws.
On October 13, 2025, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;34,964.45 ($40,450.37) and a share premium that was to be determined by our board of directors in accordance with the criteria set by our shareholders.
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 3,496,445 class C shares, class X-2 shares, or a combination of the two.
Subscriptions were to be completed by December 31, 2025.
This share capital increase was partially executed, and 2,627,242 class C shares and 424,131 class X-2 shares were issued at a subscription price of &euro;76.32 ($88.29) per share (&euro;1,526.40 or $1,765.89 without giving effect to the March 2024 stock split).
On March 23, 2026, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights, which was amended on April 23, 2026, and on May 28, 2026, in the context of the April 2026 stock split and the May 2026 reverse stock split described below.
The share capital increase was issued with a maximum aggregate nominal amount of &euro;52,410.90 ($60,775.68) and a maximum aggregate share premium of &euro;39,947,587.98 ($46,323,223.02), corresponding to a subscription price of &euro;15.26 ($17.70) per share (&euro;1,526.40 or $1,770.01 without giving effect to the April 2026 stock split and the May 2026 reverse stock split).
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 2,620,545 class X-2 shares (adjusted to reflect the April 2026 stock split and the May 2026 reverse stock split).
Subscriptions were to be completed by June 23, 2026.
On June 6, 2026, our shareholders canceled this share capital increase in full.
On April 23, 2026, our shareholders approved a 10-for-1 stock split through a share capital increase for no consideration with an aggregate nominal amount of &euro;12,123,744.48 ($14,177,506.79).
The increase authorized the issuance of 558,433,233 class A shares, 56,822,778 class B shares, 288,132,219 class C shares, 136,576,530 class X-1 shares, 52,436,448 class X-2   162 Table of Contents   shares, and 119,973,240 class X-3 shares with no par value.
The share capital increase was executed, and a total of 1,212,374,448 shares were issued to existing shareholders for no consideration and in proportion to their holdings as of the issuance date, with an implicit par value per share of &euro;0.01.
On May 28, 2026, our shareholders approved a 1-for-2 reverse stock split, reducing the number of existing shares as of the date of the resolution from 1,347,082,720 to 673,541,360, with an implicit par value per share of &euro;0.02.
On June 6, 2026, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights.
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 56,240 class X-1 shares, class X-2 shares, or a combination of the two, or, as of the effective date of this registration statement, ordinary shares.
Subscriptions are to be completed by December 31, 2030.
The increase is reserved for certain warrants granted to some of our directors under the warrant terms approved by our board of directors on November 21, 2024 (and amended on December 18, 2025).
The maximum number of shares that may be issued under this share capital increase will be adjusted for any stock split or reverse stock split.
On June 6, 2026, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights.
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 51,000,000 class X-1 shares, class X-2 shares, or a combination of the two, or, as of the effective date of this registration statement, ordinary shares.
Subscriptions are to be completed by December 31, 2046.
These shares will be offered to the participants of the 2026 Equity Compensation Plan in accordance with its terms.
The maximum number of shares that may be issued under this share capital increase will be adjusted for any stock split or reverse stock split.
On June 6, 2026, our shareholders approved a divisible and incremental share capital increase with the exclusion of preemption rights.
The increase authorized the issuance of up to 120,200,000 ordinary shares for this offering.
The maximum number of ordinary shares that may be issued under this share capital increase will be adjusted for any stock split or reverse stock split.
Subscriptions are to be completed by December 31, 2026.
As of immediately before this offering, our share capital amounts to &euro;13,470,827.20 and is divided into 673,541,360 shares with no par value, comprised of: &#8226; 310,240,685 class A shares &#8203; &#8226; 31,568,210 class B shares &#8203; &#8226; 160,073,455 class C shares &#8203; &#8226; 75,875,850 class X-1 shares &#8203; &#8226; 29,131,360 class X-2 shares &#8203; &#8226; 66,651,800 class X-3 shares &#8203; On April 23, 2026, our shareholders approved our Bylaws, which implement the Share Conversion and which, together with the Share Conversion, will become effective upon the effective date of this registration statement.
As a result, upon the effectiveness of this registration statement, our share capital will consist of two classes of shares: ordinary shares and class A shares.
All our issued shares, including treasury shares and outstanding shares, are fully paid.
  163 Table of Contents   Bylaws The following is a summary of the material provisions of our Bylaws as they relate to the material terms of our shares.
Corporate purpose Our corporate purpose includes the following activities, which may be carried out directly or indirectly through our affiliates, in Italy and abroad: &#8226; Acquiring, holding, managing, monetizing, and disposing of equity interests, businesses, business units, and assets &#8203; &#8226; Acquiring, conceiving, designing, developing, producing, managing, maintaining, promoting, commercializing, distributing, supplying, licensing, using, monetizing, and disposing of: &#8203; &#9702; Hardware and software, including third-party hardware and software (where applicable), such as applications (for browsers, computers, and mobile devices), artificial intelligence models, automation tools, and data analysis technologies &#8203; &#9702; Other products and services (both physical and digital), including third-party products and services (where applicable), such as digital platforms and marketplaces, e-commerce websites, and system integration and advisory services &#8203; &#9702; Intellectual and industrial property rights, including those of third parties (where applicable), such as authors' rights and related rights, copyright, trademarks, patents, trade secrets, sui generis rights on databases, and designs and models &#8203; &#9702; Instrumental and ancillary commercial, industrial, movable property, real estate, and financial transactions that our board of directors may consider useful to achieve our corporate purpose &#8203; Applicable provisions Upon the effectiveness of this registration statement, we will be subject to both the provisions of the Italian Civil Code that apply to joint stock companies and to companies listed on a regulated market ( societ&#224; che fanno ricorso al mercato del capitale di rischio ).
Classes of shares Following this offering, our share capital will amount to &euro;14,158,800 and will be divided into 707,940,000 shares (assuming no exercise of the underwriters' option to purchase additional ordinary shares from us), with no par value, of which: &#8226; 397,699,315 are ordinary shares (including treasury shares) &#8203; &#8226; 310,240,685 are class A shares &#8203; Only ordinary shares will be traded.
Our share capital may be increased through contributions of cash, assets in kind, and receivables.
We may also issue shares with rights other than those of the shares already issued, in accordance with our Bylaws and applicable law.
Authority to increase the share capital Under article 2443 of the Italian Civil Code, our shareholders may authorize our board of directors to increase our share capital and to issue bonds convertible into shares up to a specified amount and for a period that may not exceed five years from the date of the shareholder resolution approving such increase or issuance.
Under Italian law, shareholders generally have preemption rights to subscribe for newly issued shares in proportion to their existing holdings.
These rights may be waived or limited by the bylaws or by an extraordinary shareholder meeting, subject to certain conditions.
Under article 2441, paragraph 4, second sentence, of the Italian Civil Code, preemption rights may be excluded for up to 10% of the pre-existing share capital, on the condition that the subscription price per share be equal to the market value of an ordinary share, as confirmed by an independent auditor.
  164 Table of Contents   We may issue bonds, either convertible or non-convertible into shares, in accordance with our Bylaws and applicable law.
We may also issue participating and non-participating financial instruments, either convertible or non-convertible into shares, as well as warrants and other financial instruments, in accordance with our Bylaws and applicable law.
Subject to our Bylaws and applicable law, we may receive funding from our shareholders through capital contributions (with or without the issuance of equity or other consideration) and shareholder loans (interest-bearing or non-interest-bearing and repayable in accordance with their terms).
On June 6, 2026, subject to and with effect as of the effective date of this registration statement, our shareholders authorized our board of directors to take one or more of the following actions on one or more occasions during the five-year period beginning on that date: &#8226; Increase our share capital through the issuance of ordinary shares.
The increase may be divisible and incremental, with or without consideration.
&#8203; &#8226; Issue convertible bonds or non-participating financial instruments that entitle the holder to subscribe for ordinary shares, increasing our share capital accordingly.

The increase may be divisible and incremental, but must be for cash consideration.
